title: Annickia chlorantha
taxonomy: Annickia chlorantha is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family comprises over 160 genera and 2,200 species of flowering plants. The Annonaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Members of the Annonaceae family are known for their diverse range of appearances and growth habits, from small shrubs to large trees. Annickia chlorantha, in particular, is a species of tree that is native to tropical Africa.

In traditional medicine, the bark and roots of the plant are used to treat various health conditions. intro: Annickia chlorantha, also known as the African Mahogany tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, as well as its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Annickia chlorantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
